notes.note: SOMETIMES THE SO CAN BE INCONSISTANT. WE'VE SEEN THIS BEFORE WITH UNPUNCHED OUTLET OVERRIDING PUNCHED. WHILE ITS GOOD TO OVERRIDE WITH THE LESS COMMON THING THAT APPEARS ON THE SO. WE SHOULD FLAG THIS  FOR THE USER THAT THERES AN INCONSISTANCY WITH THE SALES ORDER. THIS TIME THOUGH, THE FAULT IS ON THE SIDE OF THE SALES ORDER. NOT OUR DATA COLLECTION. WE WANT TO FLAG THIS FOR THE USER SO THEY CAN BRING IT UP TO SALES AND GET THE SALES ORDER FIXED SO ITS CONSISTANT
notes.resolution: Explicit outlet-flange removal now selects flanged NO and punched NO while preserving both source contradictions as engineering review flags.

notes.note: THIS IS GOOD AS LONG AS EVERY TIME A SIMILAR NOTE COMES UP ON OTHER ORDERS, THIS GETS HANDLED THE SAME WAY
notes.resolution: Added regression coverage proving hyphenated and spaced silicone-free caulk wording maps identically to SPECIAL CONSTRUCTION caulking.

notes.note: I WANT YOU TO LOOK AT ORDER 421949, IT HAS A VERY SIMILAR NOTE (LOOKS IDENTICAL) NOW THE PARSER DIDN’T GROUP IT EXACTLY HOW I WANTED IT, BUT IM HANDLING THAT IN THIS NOTES CHECK.
